title: Jumellea liliodora
taxonomy: Jumellea liliodora is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This species is a type of epiphytic orchid native to Madagascar. Jumellea liliodora is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Jumellea is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to the genus Aerangis. Within the family Orchidaceae, Jumellea liliodora is also classified as a member of the tribe Vandeae.
uses: Jumellea liliodora is reportedly used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand and validate its medicinal uses. However, its potential applications in traditional medicine make it an area of interest for further study.

side_effects: Jumellea liliodora is an orchid species that has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains compounds that can cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and vomiting, if ingested. Ingestion of the plant's compounds can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and other systemic problems. Additionally, Jumellea liliodora's sap may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. It is essential to exercise caution and avoid handling the plant excessively to minimize potential side effects.
